description_long: This lamppost features a tall, slender, and ornate black metal pole with a robust, hexagonal base for stability. At the top, it branches into three distinct lantern-style lamp heads, each encased in a clear, faceted glass housing with decorative finials on top. The central pole is adorned with subtle ornamental rings and moldings, giving it a classic Victorian or Edwardian streetlamp appearance. The lamp heads are symmetrically arranged, providing wide illumination coverage, and the glass panels are slightly tinted, enhancing the vintage aesthetic. The base is solid and slightly flared, suggesting it is designed to be securely mounted into the ground. Overall, the lamppost exudes an elegant, historical charm suitable for upscale urban parks, boulevards, or heritage districts.
description: A tall, ornate black lamppost with three lantern-style glass lamp heads and decorative Victorian detailing, mounted on a sturdy hexagonal base.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three lantern heads clearly, with the central head facing forward and the ornate base fully visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the side profile of the leftmost lamp head and the depth of the central pole's decorative features.
description_view_2: Rear view with the backs of the three lamp heads visible, showing the symmetry and the full width of the base.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, emphasizing the rightmost lamp head and the ornamental details on the pole.
attribute_lamp_head_design: Victorian lantern-style with faceted clear glass panels and decorative finials
